page: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and passmark CPU score. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 4505.69 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds at 98.5 GB/sec. Redis SET operations perform exceptionally well at 73.84 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 45.88 GB/sec. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with gzip compression rated as weak at 23.21 GB/sec, and LLM text generation speeds are poor, dropping to 5.9 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5n.24xlarge offers high memory density and high-speed network throughput, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that require substantial network bandwidth without the overhead of local NVMe drives. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the resource allocation entirely on memory capacity and network capability. This profile is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a 3.1 GHz clock speed and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with Redis SET operations reaching 73.84 million operations per second. Conversely, single-threaded gzip compression and LLM text generation speeds are weak. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput network applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores, high-speed network bandwidth, and substantial memory capacity for demanding database workloads.
